partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses critical details about the purpose of tuning (adjusting for workloads/database sizes), the actor (on-site personnel), and the specific location (base or data processing megacenter).

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The ability to tune data management services must be available at a 'base or data processing megacenter'.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses critical details about the purpose of tuning (adjusting for workloads/database sizes), the actor (on-site personnel), and the mode of operation (fixed/static).

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: Personnel must have the ability to redefine or reset the names of connect descriptors to database server instances if a server name or location changes.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement changes the actor from 'The XS' to 'Personnel' and adds a new material condition ('if a server name or location changes').

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: Session time-out values must be able to be reset before a user session is initialized.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses the critical detail that session time-out values must also be 'user definable'.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system shall provide XS services to several different classes of users.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement generalizes 'Portion of XS services' to 'XS services' and omits the specific examples of user classes (data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers).

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: Maintenance activities shall cover planned shutdowns, upgrades, and modifications.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers some maintenance activities (shutdowns, upgrades, modifications) but omits others (open not-mounted, online/off-line implementations, other related actions) and adds 'planned' to shutdowns.

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: The XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with the existing data elements of the DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS products.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system must internally interface with the existing data elements of the DBIF.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses the 'transparent to the operator' condition and only specifies one of the three required interfaces (DBIF, DAC, COTS RDBMS).

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: The XS shall internally interface (transparent to the operator) with the existing data elements of the DBIF, DAC, and COTS RDBMS products.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: The system must internally interface with the existing data elements of the DAC.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses the 'transparent to the operator' condition and only specifies one of the three required interfaces (DBIF, DAC, COTS RDBMS).

partial_only_examples.source_requirement: OTHER REQUIREMENTS None PACKAGING REQUIREMENTS The XS software shall be delivered in accordance with DII COE guidelines.
partial_only_examples.generated_requirement: As a general rule, all XS components for the DII COE must adhere to the compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS.
partial_only_examples.reason: The generated requirement shifts from 'delivery in accordance with DII COE guidelines' to 'adherence to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS' for DII COE components, losing the 'delivery' aspect and adding specific compliance details.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Each voucher slip should contain the bar code of the Voucher with two identifications one for the client and the other for the partner.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: Each voucher slip should have a bar code with two identifications, one for the client and one for the partner.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully covers the source, preserving all critical details about the barcode and its two identifications.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The authorized user can alter / amend the Claim if required.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: Only an authorized user can alter or amend a claim.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully captures that only an authorized user can alter or amend a claim, which is the core intent of the source. The 'if required' condition from the source is a contextual detail for when the action happens, not who can perform it.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: During every distribution transaction the user should select the name of the sales man listed from Team Master.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: During every distribution transaction, the user must select the name of the salesman from the Team Master list.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully covers the source, preserving all critical details including the action, actor, context, and data source.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: This validity date can be amended or altered at the system level ONLY by the authorized user Voucher will also have MRP (maximum retail price) Voucher should have three tear off portioned slips with a sub-section tear-off for the partner.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The voucher validity date can only be amended or altered at the system level by an authorized user.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement accurately and completely captures the detail about the voucher validity date being amended or altered only by an authorized user at the system level.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The Salesman master should capture following information’s.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The 'Salesman master' record must capture all necessary information for each person.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully covers the source, interpreting 'following information’s' as 'all necessary information' which is a reasonable and faithful interpretation given the source's vagueness.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Patient Status - (Select from list of status options populating from Patient Status Master) suppose the status is Referral then the system will have the provision to capture Referred To and Reason for Referral, the reason for Referral should be populated from Referral master.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The `Reason for Referral` must be populated from the `Referral master`.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement faithfully captures the detail that 'Reason for Referral' must be populated from 'Referral master'.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The VSP Master Information screen should have a provision to enter the payment terms agreed between MSIU and VSP.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system needs a place to enter payment terms agreed upon between MSIU and the VSP, which are then displayed on the VSP Master Information screen.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement alters the location of the entry provision, implying the entry might not be directly on the VSP Master Information screen, and adds a display aspect.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: During the claim entry the system should capture the following information.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: During the claim entry process, the system must capture the necessary information about what's happening.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement generalizes 'the following information' to 'the necessary information about what's happening', losing specificity.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: If the claim from same VSP having more than two times of Thump Impression mismatching, then the system should automatically produce a warning message and the same time the system should In-Activate the VSP.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: If a claim from the same VSP has a thumb impression mismatch more than two times, the system must in-activate that VSP.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement misses one of the two critical actions specified in the source: producing a warning message.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The system admin only can able to create User Groups The system should have the provision to create any number of individual user under any user group.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: Only a system admin can create User Groups.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement covers the first part of the source (system admin creating user groups) but completely omits the second part about creating individual users under groups.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: VSP Name Staff Code Staff Name Staff Type Qualification The system will generate message while creating a new staff with existing name, but the system will allow the user to save that new staff if its required.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The system must allow a user to save a new staff member with an existing name if required.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement only covers the saving aspect and omits the critical detail about generating a message when creating a new staff with an existing name.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Each voucher should have two portions with three tear off voucher slips each for Client and Partner.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: One of the voucher's tear-off slips must have a sub-section tear-off for the partner.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement significantly alters the described physical structure of the voucher, changing 'two portions with three tear off voucher slips each for Client and Partner' to 'one of the voucher's tear-off slips must have a sub-section tear-off for the partner'.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: The principles of the voucher, the contents of the health care services, their required quality, the standards to be met by the providers and their infrastructure, as well as the measures for monitoring the quality have been defined in the PDS.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: All rules for the voucher, including health care services and quality standards for providers, are defined in the PDS.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement generalizes several specific details from the source, such as 'required quality' of services, 'infrastructure' for providers, and completely omits 'measures for monitoring the quality'.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: While entering the No of vouchers required, the system should automatically pick the Batch No’s with voucher No’s from the available voucher stock and list the details of each voucher with below information’s as a grid format.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: The receipt needs to automatically list the details for each voucher used, pulling the `Batch No` and `voucher No` from the available voucher stock.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement loses the condition 'While entering the No of vouchers required' and the display format 'as a grid format' mentioned in the source.

validator_match_examples.source_requirement: Invoice amount = No of vouchers x Wholesale price Mode of payment is Cash The mandatory information required during a distribution transaction is mentioned below.
validator_match_examples.generated_requirement: For transactions where the invoice amount is the number of vouchers multiplied by the wholesale price, the mode of payment is always Cash.
validator_match_examples.reason: The generated requirement makes the cash payment conditional on the invoice calculation, which is not explicitly stated in the source. It also completely omits the part about mandatory information.
